motion: Nutation, counter-nutation. A few degrees of rotation.
positions: IS IR/ER (posterior inlet, p.5), SI IR/ER (posterior outlet, p.5)
role: IS describes ilium moving on sacrum (posterior inlet, p.8). SI describes sacrum moving on ilium (posterior outlet, p.10). Same physical joint, two PRI reference frames.

motion: Minimal — a few mm of shear and compression during gait, pregnancy, single-leg stance.
positions: IP IR/ER (anterior inlet, p.5), IsP IR/ER (anterior outlet, p.5)
role: PRI uses the pubic symphysis as a positional landmark. IP describes ilium relative to pubis (inlet). IsP describes ischium relative to pubis (outlet).

positions: AF IR/ER (pelvis on femur, p.5), FA IR/ER (femur on pelvis, p.5)
role: AF = closed chain (stance leg, pelvis moves on planted femur). FA = open chain (swing leg, femur moves on pelvis).
Not named in PRI’s position system. Psoas and diaphragmatic crura attach here but the joint involves spinal motion, not innominate displacement.
Not named. Coccyx position is implicit in outlet mechanics (SI IR/ER).
